sourceMovie.title: Urotsukidōji: Legend of the Overfiend
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Urotsukidōji: Legend of the Overfiend (1989) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ninja Scroll
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Urotsukidōji: Legend of the Overfiend clicked for you, Ninja Scroll (1993) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and action, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Attack on Titan: Crimson Bow and Arrow

Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Urotsukidōji: Legend of the Overfiend?
faq.answer: Try Ninja Scroll, Attack on Titan: Crimson Bow and Arrow, Attack on Titan: Wings of Freedom—each shares DNA with Urotsukidōji: Legend of the Overfiend while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Urotsukidōji: Legend of the Overfiend?
faq.answer: Urotsukidōji: Legend of the Overfiend (1989) is best described as Animation, Action, Horror, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Urotsukidōji: Legend of the Overfiend part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Urotsukidōji Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
